channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A sweeping vision for the Navy is colliding with harsh shipbuilding realities
item.description: "Think of U.S. shipbuilding as an enterprise, facing enterprise-wide challenges," Seamus Daniels said. "This is a systemic problem with many interwoven issues." The post A sweeping vision for the Navy is colliding with harsh shipbuilding realities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: As Tesla ​reports smallest profit since pandemic, company plans to shift Fremont factory to production of Optimus robots
item.description: CEO Elon Musk says the company will close down production of two older car models, S and X, and convert the Fremont factory to produce its Optimus robots instead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Audi declares war on ‘giant iPad’ dashboards – as design boss signals return to the analog buttons everyone’s craving
item.description: Audi's design boss sees a return to analog switches and dials for the brand, as massive screens aren't the answer.
item.dc:creator: Leon Poultney, TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft beats Wall Street expectations with $81.3B revenue
item.description: Microsoft reports revenue of $81.3 billion for the October-December quarter, marking a 17% increase from the previous year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A sea turtle with 3 flippers swims free after Florida rehab, now followed by satellite
item.description: A loggerhead sea turtle has returned to the Atlantic Ocean after rehabilitation at a Florida turtle hospital.
item.dc:creator: Cody Jackson And Freida Frisaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: WhatsApp gets new security features
item.description: The popular messaging app WhatsApp got a range of new security features Tuesday with the launch of “Strict Account Settings.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Three reasons that should drive your endpoint management strategy in 2026
item.description: 2026 will be the year many government IT teams either implement their first unified endpoint management (UEM) strategy or replace outdated ones entirely. The post Three reasons that should drive your endpoint management strategy in 2026 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: 9 startups from Palantir alumni you need to know
item.description: Last week, I published a deep exploration into Palantir and its founder factory and how the company’s power and success can be explained by its ability to attract elite talent and how it empowers them to develop their skills and learn new ones in the projects they pursue.
item.dc:creator: David Lidsky, Fast Company

item.title: How two companies are moving AI prototypes to production
item.description: With many AI projects failing, there’s no one-size-fits-all formula for advancing AI proofs of concept to real-world use in the corporate world. But two companies, Ernst & Young (EY) and Lumen, have had success — though they’ve tackled the issue in dramatically different ways.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google adds AI image generation to Chrome browser, side panel option for virtual assistant
item.description: Google is empowering its Chrome browser with the ability to alter pictures and introducing a side panel for a virtual assistant to help with online tasks as part of its push to turbocharge its of digital services with more artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Spanish soccer league offers reward of 50 euros for tips to ID bars illegally broadcasting games
item.description: The Spanish league says it is stepping up its fight against audio-visual fraud by offering 50 euros for each verified tip on the establishments broadcasting games without proper permission.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Amazon confirms 16,000 job cuts, including to AWS
item.description: Amazon is cutting about 16,000 jobs across the company, SVP of People Experience and Technology Beth Galetti wrote in an email to employees Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How secure is your business really? New survey claims 90% of enterprise AI systems could be breached within 90 minutes
item.description: Businesses are rushing to introduce AI, leaving the back doors wide open for cybercriminals and nation-state actors.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: Meta-Corning fiber deal signals a new bottleneck in AI infrastructure
item.description: Meta has announced a $6 billion, multi-year fiber supply agreement with Corning, highlighting how AI infrastructure constraints are shifting beyond compute and into the physical network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: It's not Dante's Inferno 2, but this upcoming indie game takes inspiration from the same classic poem and could be the sequel to Visceral's 2010 game I've been hoping for all these years
item.description: Upcoming indie game The Alighieri Circle Dante's Bloodline looks like an intriguing psychological thriller, and taking its inspiration from the classic poem, The Divine Comedy, has its hooks in me already.
item.dc:creator: Rob Dwiar, TechRadar

item.title: France just approved a social media ban for kids under 15
item.description: French lawmakers approved a bill banning social media for children under 15, paving the way for the measure to enter into force at the start of the next school year in September, as the idea of setting a minimum age for use of the platforms gains momentum across Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: French authorities to ban Teams, Zoom, other video apps for gov’t use
item.description: France plans to phase out American video conferencing services such as Zoom and Microsoft Teams within government agencies and replace them with its own proprietary tool, Visio.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Disruptive innovation is key to building world-changing companies, but it needs a moral compass in the age of AI
item.description: At the Consumer Electronics Show in early January, Razer made waves by unveiling a small jar containing a holographic anime bot designed to accompany gamers not just during gameplay, but in daily life.
item.dc:creator: Julia Freeland Fisher, Fast Company

item.title: Uber unveils a new robotaxi with no driver behind the wheel
item.description: Uber robotaxi testing begins on public roads in San Francisco Bay Area with plans to launch driverless rides in a major U.S. city later in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alibaba’s Qwen3-Max-Thinking expands enterprise AI model choices
item.description: Alibaba Cloud’s latest AI model, Qwen3-Max-Thinking, is staking a claim as one of the world’s most advanced reasoning engines after posting benchmark results that delivered competitive results against leading models from Google and OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft launches its second generation AI inference chip, Maia 200
item.description: Signaling that the future of AI may not just be how many tokens an AI model generates, but how optimally it does so, Microsoft has announced Maia 200, which it described as a breakthrough inference accelerator and inference powerhouse.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Kotlin-based Ktor 3.4 boosts HTTP client handling
item.description: JetBrains has released Ktor 3.4, an update to its Kotlin-based framework for asynchronous server-side and client-side application development.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Zero-trust data governance needed to protect AI models from slop
item.description: Organizations need to be less trustful of data given how much of it is AI-generated, according to new research from Gartner.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: European Union opens investigation into Musk's AI chatbot Grok over sexual deepfakes
item.description: European Union regulators have opened a formal investigation into Elon Musk's social media platform X after its AI chatbot Grok started producing nonconsensual sexualized deepfake images.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2
